opp.name: Friesland_Upstream - 2025
opp.next_step: 1 April 2026: Caroline got a mail from Florencia: Hi Caroline, Thank you again for the invitation. I found the sessions and the networking especially insightful—great conversations and excellent speeches. It was also very nice to learn more about Zycus’ capabilities. I agree it would be valuable to continue the conversation and involve a broader group of stakeholders on our side. At the same time, the coming weeks are quite busy internally, with several initiatives running in parallel. This makes it a bit challenging to coordinate calendars, especially as colleagues are not all based in the Netherlands. Let me please align internally on who to involve and revert shortly on potential availability on our side. Thank you, Florencia 20 Feb 2026: invited to Horizon 28/10/2025: Confidence level 20% 24/10/205: SRM and Merlin Intake Demo for Florencia & Turgut - well received - very early stage 02/10/2025: Invite for a meeting at DPW 23/09/25: Demo set up October 6. 15/09/25: SPRM Demo Proposal to Florencia 22/08/2025: Caroline had a really good call with Florencia - notes below - next step onsite demo Dear Florencia , Thank you for taking the time to meet with us today. It was a pleasure to connect and discuss the potential collaboration between Zycus and FrieslandCampina. Meeting Summary: During our introductory call, we had the opportunity to learn more about FrieslandCampina's current procurement landscape and your interest in exploring new solutions to enhance efficiency and streamline processes. We discussed Zycus’ capabilities, particularly focusing on our composable procurement platform, Merlin AI, and how it can integrate seamlessly with your existing systems, like SAP and Coupa. It was insightful to hear about your current challenges and goals, especially regarding supplier management and digital procurement. Action Items/Next Steps: 1. Share Demo Materials: I will send over a shared space with detailed information and a demo of our guided intake solution to give you a clearer picture of how Zycus can meet your needs - please open this link https://zycus.showpad.biz/s/zycus-guided-intake 2. Schedule a Demo Session: Let's aim to schedule a demo session for the SPRM tool, @Akash Kulwal will support with scheduling 3. Implementation Discussion: As you explore the potential of integrating new solutions, we can further discuss the feasibility of parallel implementation with your ongoing S4HANA project to ensure a smooth transition I have checked with our implementation Head : W e can definitely do a parallel implementation but we need to stagger our golive by 2-3 months from S4H go live I look forward to continuing our conversation and exploring how Zycus can support FrieslandCampina in achieving its procurement objectives. 13/08/2025: Discovery call set up with Procurement Excellence person - unhappy Coupa clients 27/1/25: Intel KPMG - new CPO starting, S4Hana implementation staring and not happy with Coupa - we have an opportunity her through KPMG 7/1/2024: demo request for a high level overview Demo with the team. KPMG is the aggregator in between
opp.ais_why:
• Aug 2025 Avoma notes describe Florencia Somaini as 'FrieslandCampina's Procurement Excellence Manager, leading the digital procurement roadmap to leverage advanced AI technologies' ‚Äî a verbatim Avoma capture of a buyer-side champion whose role is explicitly AI-roadmap-driven. The 3-year transformation framing and demo plan including 'supplier risk management and the Merlin intake solution' anchor AI as the strategic agenda. Deal scope explicitly includes ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake.
• Formal Evaluation, BD Engaged; champion is the Procurement Excellence Manager (with 15 years of Coupa experience now driving the AI roadmap) ‚Äî a credible cross-platform comparator. Multiple meetings across Nov 2024 to Aug 2025; S/4HANA go-live mid-2026 creates integration urgency. Last activity Apr 2026. Florencia's previous Coupa background means she can compare Zycus's AI capabilities credibly against an alternative.
• High confidence: SF Description plus rich Avoma evidence with a named AI-driven roadmap owner whose role title literally references advanced AI technologies. Held at 9 (not 10) because no formal AI-mandating RFI/RFP has been issued yet ‚Äî it's a strategic roadmap exploration rather than a competitive shortlist. A POC commitment would push to 10.

items.quote: this is really not an ask from management... we are just exploring.
we need to discuss it internally... there are some internal processes.

items.area: No buyer call in the last 60 days (buyer silent since 1 April 2026)
items.quote: Let me please align internally... and revert shortly.

metrics.narrative: The pain is well articulated but never quantified into a savings or ROI case. On the 22 Aug 2025 introductory call Florencia Somaini described roughly 2.4 billion in annual spend, an expanding set of buying channels and users 'completely lost' trying to find the right buying channel in Coupa, plus a fragmented tool estate (Coupa for P2P, SAP Ariba and Keelvar for sourcing, Salesforce for supplier management which she called 'not a real supplier management tool'). No cost, cycle-time or headcount number was attached to any of this, and the effort was framed as exploratory ('we are just exploring'). Quantifying the SRM business case against the 2.4 billion spend is the move.

champion.narrative: Florencia Somaini is a genuine but stalling champion. She initiated and drove the conversation, brings 15 years of procurement-tech and Coupa implementation experience (a credible cross-platform comparator), attended the 24 Oct 2025 SRM and Merlin Intake demo which was 'well received', accepted the Horizon invitation and on 1 April 2026 wrote that it was 'valuable to continue the conversation and involve a broader group of stakeholders'. What has NOT developed is her ability to mobilize: 93 days after promising to align internally she has not reverted, has not introduced any economic buyer, and lacks budget authority. She is a developing, now-cooling champion who advocates but cannot yet convert exploration into a funded, multi-threaded deal.

competition.narrative: There is no active named software rival winning the SRM evaluation; the real competition is inertia. The status quo is a fragmented incumbent stack (Coupa for P2P for 8+ years, SAP Ariba and Keelvar for sourcing, Salesforce for supplier management) and, above all, the S4HANA go-live consuming the roadmap and budget. Florencia was explicit that they are 'not looking to replace Coupa' but want to put solutions 'on top', and that they are 'also considering building something in-house' with an internal AI team and IT involved. The Zycus wedge is that she is dissatisfied with Coupa usability and rates the Zycus AI/Merlin story highly (Heineken reference resonated); winning means neutralizing the S4HANA integration fear and out-positioning an in-house build on time-to-value.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is real and clearly owned by Florencia Somaini. On 22 Aug 2025 she described users 'completely lost' with a proliferation of buying channels, a 'mess' of point tools, and Salesforce being used for supplier management despite 'not being a real supplier management tool', which she said 'yes, we are looking to maybe replace'. She also noted most Coupa users are infrequent (one or two requisitions a year) and need a far simpler experience. The pain is concentrated in supplier relationship/risk management and guided intake, which maps directly to the Zycus iSupplier, iRisk and Merlin Intake scope. It is confirmed pain, just not yet quantified.

sources: Avoma introductory call 22 Aug 2025 (Caroline Lacocque, Florencia Somaini)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown: no MSA/SOW, legal, procurement-channel or InfoSec conversation has taken place, consistent with a deal still in exploration. The only paper-relevant signal is timing, not process: the S4HANA go-live targeted for mid-2026 acts as a forcing function that currently works AGAINST Zycus, since Florencia's stated worry is integration rework if an SRM project runs in parallel. Caroline offered a staggered approach (go-live 2 to 3 months after S4HANA) on 22 Aug 2025, but nothing has been formalized. Visibility would come from a written parallel-implementation and integration plan tied to the S4HANA schedule.

sources: SF Contact Roles (Jean-Olivier Quentin, Felipe Brollo, Florencia Somaini)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has ever been engaged. The buying activity sits entirely with Florencia Somaini (Procurement Excellence Manager), who is driving the digital-procurement roadmap but is operational and does not control budget; on 22 Aug 2025 she positioned the work as exploration rather than a funded initiative. The Salesforce contact roles name credible budget owners, Jean-Olivier Quentin (Global Director, Procurement and Chief People Officer) and Felipe Brollo (Global Indirects Procurement Director), but neither appears on any call, task or event. Identifying and reaching a real economic buyer through Florencia is the single biggest gate on this deal.

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is running is a slow internal assessment with no defined evaluation gates or timeline. Florencia said they would 'start with one module' (SRM), then expand, and that 'there are some internal processes, we need to discuss it internally' before involving a broader group; on 1 April 2026 she committed to 'align internally on who to involve and revert shortly' but never did. No RFI/RFP, no shortlist, no committee and no approval chain has been named, and the reverting-back to Qualified stage on 1 Jul 2026 confirms the process has not advanced. Mapping who decides and forcing a concrete evaluation timeline is required.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging from an internal SRM assessment rather than a formal RFP. On 22 Aug 2025 Florencia said they were 'in an assessment instance right now for their SRM tool' and 'trying to gather the requirements to understand exactly what we need', naming ease of use for infrequent users, AI capability, and clean integration with SAP and the in-flight S4HANA program as the things that matter. The 15 Jan 2025 demo surfaced concrete interest in supplier information/risk, AI-assisted sourcing and contract risk scoring. These are buyer-stated but not yet locked into a documented, weighted scorecard, so criteria are confirmed in theme but partial in formality.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — No competitor is preferred over Zycus; the strongest current threat is do-nothing inertia driven by the mid-2026 S4HANA go-live, which is freezing new procurement-tool decisions and reinforcing a 93-day buyer silence. The second threat is an in-house build using FrieslandCampina's internal AI team (IT-led, control, no S4HANA integration risk). Coupa is the entrenched P2P incumbent that Florencia does not intend to replace but is dissatisfied with on usability, which is Zycus's wedge; SAP Ariba, Keelvar and Salesforce supplier-connect are incumbents in adjacent scopes, with Salesforce being the SRM tool the buyer explicitly wants to replace.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: No competitor is preferred over Zycus; the strongest current threat is do-nothing inertia driven by the mid-2026 S4HANA go-live, which is freezing new procurement-tool decisions and reinforcing a 93-day buyer silence.

bullets.text: S4HANA go-live targeted mid-2026 is freezing new procurement-tool decisions and is the stated reason not to change now.
No economic buyer engaged; deal driven solely by an operational champion with no budget authority.
Effort framed as exploration, not a management ask; no budget or funded initiative confirmed.
In-house build with an internal AI team is a live IT-led alternative that avoids new-vendor and integration risk.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 14 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 11 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (14), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 11 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 9/Strong and the calls corroborate genuine AI appetite: the champion explicitly leads an AI-driven digital-procurement roadmap and praised Zycus's AI leadership. Not over-rated; the constraint is buying process and timing, not AI interest.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (users lost in Coupa, fragmented tools, Salesforce not a real SRM tool) against roughly 2.4 billion annual spend; no ROI, savings or cycle-time figures quantified.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum has reversed. After a well-received Oct 2025 demo and a Feb/Mar 2026 Horizon touch, the buyer went quiet on 1 April 2026, a rep-side roundtable invite in May drew no reply, and on 1 July 2026 the owner regressed the stage from Formal Evaluation back to Qualified.

critical_signals.text: The buyer has not engaged since the 1 April Horizon follow-up and the evaluation was quietly rolled back to an earlier stage on 1 July, signalling the exploration has stalled.
No rival is winning; the deal is losing to inertia because the mid-2026 S4HANA go-live is freezing any new procurement-tool decision.
The initiative is still framed as exploration with no budget owner and no numbers attached to the roughly 2.4 billion spend, so there is nothing to justify a purchase yet.
A fresh thread was opened to Vincent Bremond in Global Category Procurement via a May roundtable invite, but he has not yet responded.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling toward dark: about 93 days of buyer silence, a rep outreach with no reply, and an owner-driven stage regression.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 24 Nov 2024, roughly 586 days old, in Qualified for essentially its whole life (Formal Evaluation 28 Aug 2025 to 1 Jul 2026, then reverted to Qualified). Close 31 Dec 2026, about 181 days out. Last buyer touch 1 April 2026 (Florencia email), about 93 days ago; last Salesforce activity 21 May 2026 was a rep-side roundtable invite to Vincent Bremond with no reply. Close date already slipped twice (31 Mar 2026 to 30 Jun 2026 on 1 Dec 2025, then 30 Jun 2026 to 31 Dec 2026 on 20 Feb 2026). To close by 31 Dec the buyer would have to traverse Shortlisted, Vendor Selected, Negotiation and Contract in about 6 months with no EB, no scheduled demo and no formal evaluation timeline: not credible on current pace.
evidence: Engagement COLD: last buyer touch 1 April 2026 Florencia email (about 93 days ago); the only later activity is a 20 to 21 May 2026 rep-initiated roundtable invitation to Vincent Bremond that drew no reply (source: SF activities 2026-05-20/21, 2026-04-01)
Owner regressed the deal: StageName Formal Evaluation to Qualified on 1 Jul 2026 by Caroline Lacocque, an honest downgrade that the evaluation stalled (source: SF field history 2026-07-01)

Florencia email 1 April 2026 promised to 'align internally on who to involve and revert shortly', with no revert, no new stakeholders and no demo scheduled in the ~12 weeks since (source: SF Next_Step__c, 2026-04-01)

No Economic Buyer engaged on any call, contact role activity or task; Florencia Somaini is Procurement Excellence Manager (operational), and senior procurement leaders Jean-Olivier Quentin and Felipe Brollo remain untouched (source: SF Contact Roles; call 2025-08-22)
S4HANA go-live mid-2026 is the dominant program; Florencia flagged integration rework as her main concern about running an SRM project in parallel (source: call 2025-08-22)
Pipeline forecast at ~181 days to close while in Qualified, with two prior close-date slips and a stage regression, is indefensible
north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer has been silent for about 93 days since the 1 April 2026 Horizon follow-up, the owner herself regressed the stage from Formal Evaluation back to Qualified on 1 July 2026, and no economic buyer has ever engaged: the deal has gone cold in exploration mode.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (Omitted / no commit) until an economic buyer engages and an evaluation timeline is confirmed

flags: Multi-thread beyond Florencia now: she cannot mobilize alone, so reach Jean-Olivier Quentin or Felipe Brollo for budget authority and Nauman Malik (GPO and Transformation Lead) for the S4HANA angle.
Neutralize the S4HANA freeze and the in-house build with a concrete parallel-implementation and integration plan, turning the biggest objection into the reason to start SRM now.
Anchor a quantified SRM business case against the ~2.4 billion spend so an economic buyer has a funded reason to act rather than defer past S4HANA.
best_practice_check.summary: Cold, single-threaded, unfunded early-stage deal: the win levers are re-opening the relationship, multi-threading to power, and neutralizing the S4HANA freeze.

customer_preference.evidence: Champion Florencia (ex-Coupa) rates Zycus a front-runner in AI and found Horizon and the demos valuable ('very nice to learn more about Zycus' capabilities', 1 April 2026), but the signal is cooling with about 93 days of silence and no Zycus-specific next step requested.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is preferred over Zycus; the strongest current threat is do-nothing inertia driven by the mid-2026 S4HANA go-live, which is freezing new procurement-tool decisions and reinforcing a 93-day buyer silence. The second threat is an in-house build using FrieslandCampina's internal AI team (IT-led, control, no S4HANA integration risk). Coupa is the entrenched P2P incumbent that Florencia does not intend to replace but is dissatisfied with on usability, which is Zycus's wedge; SAP Ariba, Keelvar and Salesforce supplier-connect are incumbents in adjacent scopes, with Salesforce being the SRM tool the buyer explicitly wants to replace.

competitors.name: Status quo / do-nothing (S4HANA go-live priority)
competitors.quote: We are now implementing S4HANA... it will take 3 years to complete. So it is maybe not the right moment to change... my concern is mainly the rework in terms of integrations.
competitors.how_we_win: Convert S4HANA from blocker to wedge with a written parallel-implementation and integration plan (staggered go-live 2 to 3 months after S4H) so the buyer sees no rework risk in starting SRM now.

competitors.name: In-house build (internal AI team, IT-led)
competitors.quote: we are also considering building something in-house... we have an AI team... IT is involved, so we're exploring all possibilities.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast time-to-value and 25 years of procurement AI patents against a from-scratch build; use the Heineken Merlin reference to show a live, proven outcome the in-house team cannot match quickly.

competitors.name: Coupa (incumbent P2P platform)
competitors.quote: We have Coupa since at least 8 years ago... users are completely lost now with Coupa... we are not looking to replace Coupa right now.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with the composable land-and-expand: start on SRM/intake on top of Coupa, prove usability and AI adoption, then earn the P2P conversation after S4HANA settles.

competitors.name: Salesforce supplier-connect (incumbent SRM tool being displaced)
competitors.quote: for supplier management we have Salesforce... it's not a real supplier management tool... yes, we are looking to maybe replace it.
competitors.how_we_win: This is the beachhead: position iSupplier plus iRisk as the purpose-built replacement for the Salesforce workaround, with risk and performance unified in one place.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent sourcing)
competitors.quote: for sourcing we are using Ariba... a very long time.
competitors.how_we_win: Out of scope for the SRM beachhead; note as future displacement once Zycus is proven, do not compete on it now.

competitors.name: Keelvar (logistics sourcing, expanding)
competitors.quote: we started to use Keelvar for logistics, and now we are expanding the use of Keelvar within the company for other categories.
competitors.how_we_win: Not competing for SRM scope; monitor its category expansion so it does not pre-empt future Zycus sourcing scope.

items.quote: it would be valuable to continue the conversation and involve a broader group of stakeholders on our side.
items.requirement: Run a broader-stakeholder SRM and Merlin Intake demo for the wider evaluation group

items.quote: I wanted to ask you what do you think... my concern is mainly the rework in terms of integrations.
items.requirement: Advise whether the SRM tool can be implemented in parallel with S4HANA without heavy integration rework

items.deliverable: Provide a written parallel-implementation plan staggering Zycus go-live 2 to 3 months after S4HANA
items.grounding_quote: I have checked with our implementation Head: we can definitely do a parallel implementation but we need to stagger our go-live by 2-3 months from S4H go-live... I will also confirm with my implementation consultant Varun.

items.deliverable: Confirm internally who to involve and share availability for the broader-group session
items.grounding_quote: Let me please align internally on who to involve and revert shortly on potential availability on our side.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin / Agentic AI story is landing well: Florencia (ex-Coupa, 15 years procurement tech) rates Zycus a front-runner in AI and the Heineken Merlin reference resonated. Scope already includes ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake. The gap is not the AI narrative but the absence of a funded buyer to act on it.

items.quote: Salesforce... it's not a real supplier management tool... we are looking to maybe replace it.
items.criterion: Purpose-built supplier risk and performance management to replace the Salesforce workaround

items.quote: most of the users going to Coupa are infrequent users... we really need something that can be used by them.
items.criterion: AI-led, easy guided intake for infrequent users

items.quote: my concern is mainly the rework in terms of integrations.
items.criterion: No integration rework risk against the in-flight S4HANA program

items.quote: this is really not an ask from management... we are just exploring.
items.source: Avoma introductory call 22 Aug 2025 and demo 15 Jan 2025
items.criterion: Funded initiative with an economic buyer and evaluation timeline

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong thematic fit for the SRM/intake beachhead and the AI narrative, but blocked by timing (S4HANA) and an unfunded, single-threaded process.

hard.ais_why:
• Aug 2025 Avoma notes describe Florencia Somaini as 'FrieslandCampina's Procurement Excellence Manager, leading the digital procurement roadmap to leverage advanced AI technologies' ‚Äî a verbatim Avoma capture of a buyer-side champion whose role is explicitly AI-roadmap-driven. The 3-year transformation framing and demo plan including 'supplier risk management and the Merlin intake solution' anchor AI as the strategic agenda. Deal scope explicitly includes ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake.
• Formal Evaluation, BD Engaged; champion is the Procurement Excellence Manager (with 15 years of Coupa experience now driving the AI roadmap) ‚Äî a credible cross-platform comparator. Multiple meetings across Nov 2024 to Aug 2025; S/4HANA go-live mid-2026 creates integration urgency. Last activity Apr 2026. Florencia's previous Coupa background means she can compare Zycus's AI capabilities credibly against an alternative.
• High confidence: SF Description plus rich Avoma evidence with a named AI-driven roadmap owner whose role title literally references advanced AI technologies. Held at 9 (not 10) because no formal AI-mandating RFI/RFP has been issued yet ‚Äî it's a strategic roadmap exploration rather than a competitive shortlist. A POC commitment would push to 10.
hard.next_step:
1 April 2026: Caroline got a mail from Florencia: Hi Caroline,

Thank you again for the invitation. I found the sessions and the networking especially insightful—great conversations and excellent speeches. It was also very nice to learn more about Zycus’ capabilities. I agree it would be valuable to continue the conversation and involve a broader group of stakeholders on our side. At the same time, the coming weeks are quite busy internally, with several initiatives running in parallel.

This makes it a bit challenging to coordinate calendars, especially as colleagues are not all based in the Netherlands. Let me please align internally on who to involve and revert shortly on potential availability on our side. Thank you, Florencia

22/08/2025: Caroline had a really good call with Florencia - notes below - next step onsite demo

Dear Florencia, Thank you for taking the time to meet with us today. 

It was a pleasure to connect and discuss the potential collaboration between Zycus and FrieslandCampina.

Meeting Summary:

During our introductory call, we had the opportunity to learn more about FrieslandCampina's current procurement landscape and your interest in exploring new solutions to enhance efficiency and streamline processes. We discussed Zycus’ capabilities, particularly focusing on our composable procurement platform, Merlin AI, and how it can integrate seamlessly with your existing systems, like SAP and Coupa. It was insightful to hear about your current challenges and goals, especially regarding supplier management and digital procurement.

Action Items/Next Steps:

1. Share Demo Materials: I will send over a shared space with detailed information and a demo of our guided intake solution to give you a clearer picture of how Zycus can meet your needs - please open this link  https://zycus.showpad.biz/s/zycus-guided-intake

2. Schedule a Demo Session: Let's aim to schedule a demo session for the SPRM tool, @Akash Kulwal will support with scheduling

3. Implementation Discussion: As you explore the potential of integrating new solutions, we can further discuss the feasibility of parallel implementation with your ongoing S4HANA project to ensure a smooth transition I have checked with our implementation Head : We can definitely do a parallel implementation but we need to stagger our golive by 2-3 months from S4H go live 

I look forward to continuing our conversation and exploring how Zycus can support FrieslandCampina in achieving its procurement objectives.
